description: Recipient-scoping is SDK-ENFORCED (ADR 0022 Option 2, §Access + §Errors): every per-notification operation takes the authenticated `recipient_usr_id` and the SDK MUST constrain the lookup to it. A caller who is not the recipient cannot reach a notification by id — `get_notification` with a non-matching `recipient_usr_id` resolves to the SAME not-found error (`NotFoundError`) as a genuinely non-existent id: no cross-recipient read path, no forbidden/denied differential (the existence-non-disclosure rule). This is the IDOR-closing vector that makes the recipient-scope guarantee conformance-enforced across all five SDKs (the `get`-vs-`list` asymmetry an adopter-gated model leaks). `runnable_today:false` until the notifications layer ships with the Option-2 (recipient-scoped) signatures.

tests.description: A notification owned by `recipient` is unreachable by `intruder`: `get_notification(recipient_usr_id={intruder}, id={not_id})` MUST raise `NotFoundError` — the SDK scopes the lookup to the supplied recipient, so a foreign caller is denied as if the notification did not exist (no cross-recipient read path).

Existence non-disclosure: `get_notification` by `intruder` on a real-but-foreign id AND by `intruder` on a genuinely non-existent id BOTH raise the SAME `NotFoundError` — no error-code/message differential that would let `intruder` infer whether the foreign notification exists.

Existence-before-state PRECEDENCE (ADR 0022 §Errors / #59): a `dismiss` by `intruder` on a notification owned by `recipient` that is already `dismissed` MUST raise `NotFoundError` — the recipient/ownership check resolves FIRST. It MUST NOT raise the terminal-state `PreconditionError`, which would leak both the foreign notification's existence AND its dismissed state. This is the mutation-path half of recipient non-disclosure (the read path is covered above) and the most-likely-mis-implemented case (a naive `dismiss` checks terminal-state before ownership).
